q: Someone cancels on you last minute:
options: Meltdown. Spiral. Done.
Annoyed but I move on fast
I check in — are they okay?
I was already making backup plans

q: How do you handle being wrong?
options: I don't handle it — I shut down
I get defensive, then feel bad later
I apologize and try to learn
I find it interesting — being wrong means growth
It explodes and I regret it
I perform calm but I'm seething
I feel it, name it, and process it
I rarely feel anger anymore — mostly sadness underneath
Want someone to fix it for me
Push through on pure willpower
Lean on my support system
Sit with it — I've learned that storms pass
A scared kid who needs reassurance
A critic who's never satisfied
A supportive friend
A calm narrator watching the story
Intensely — all or nothing
Carefully — I protect myself first
Openly — with communication and trust
Gently — I love without needing to own
Everything — I compare constantly
When someone has what I've been chasing
Rarely — but I notice it and reflect
Other people's peace of mind
I live in the moment without thinking ahead
I'm always rushing to the next thing
I try to balance present and future
I've made peace with how fast it moves

q: What do you need most from people?
options: Validation — tell me I'm okay
Respect — acknowledge what I've done
Honesty — even when it's hard
Presence — just be here

q: If you met your younger self:
options: I'd cry — they needed so much
I'd cringe a little but understand
I'd be proud of how far I've come
I'd tell them: 'You'll stop trying so hard, and that's when it gets good'
